steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The sentence has Antiquarians (noun/subject) followed by the blank, then to read (infinitive phrase), and finally might procure (main verb). The blank requires a verb form that modifies the subject without creating a second independent verb clause.

steps.title: Apply the participle rule
steps.content:
A participle (verb form ending in -ing) modifies a noun without creating a second independent clause. Hoping (C) creates the participial phrase “Antiquarians hoping to read,” leaving might procure as the sentence's main verb.
Using finite verbs like hoped, hope, or have hoped (A, B, D) would create two independent verb structures without proper coordination.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
